name: AdditionalField
description: Configuration for a single additional user field.

entries.name: name
entries.description: Field name. Used as the user object key and form input `name`.

entries.name: label
entries.description: Visible label rendered next to the input.

entries.name: prefix
entries.description: Content rendered as a prefix addon inside the input group.

entries.name: suffix
entries.description: Content rendered as a suffix addon inside the input group.

entries.name: formatOptions
entries.description:
`Intl.NumberFormat` options for number fields. Use `maximumFractionDigits`
(and optionally `minimumFractionDigits`) to allow decimals, or `style: "currency"`
/ `style: "percent"` for richer formatting.

entries.name: min
entries.description: Minimum value. Applies to `number` and `slider` input types.

entries.name: max
entries.description: Maximum value. Applies to `number` and `slider` input types.

entries.name: step
entries.description: Step value. Applies to `number` and `slider` input types.

entries.name: defaultValue
entries.description:
Default value used to seed the input on the sign-up form. On the user
profile, the value is always re-seeded from the persisted session.

entries.name: readOnly
entries.description: Render the field but exclude it from submission payloads.

entries.name: copyable
entries.description: Show a copy-to-clipboard button as a suffix. Input variant only.

entries.name: options
entries.description: Options for the select input type.

entries.name: validate
entries.description:
Custom client-side validation. Throw an `Error` (the `message` is shown
to the user) when invalid; return / resolve normally when valid.

Receives the parsed value (after `parseAdditionalFieldValue`).

entries.name: signUp
entries.description:
Render on the sign-up form. Pass `"above"` to render between the `email`
and `password` fields; otherwise the field renders below the password
block. `true` is an alias for `"below"`.

entries.name: render
entries.description:
Custom renderer. Replaces the host UI package's built-in input. Must emit
an input named `field.name` so the value is captured by the form's
`FormData`.
